title: Designing a Garden
rationale: Students design a garden and only receive a closing tasting; there is no hands-on cooking, structured Tasting, or explicitly taught kitchen safety.

title: Tastes Around the World
rationale: Although students conduct a structured sensory comparison, all foods are prepared before class and the lesson is eat-only, so the no-hands-on-cooking rule requires an empty array. No kitchen-safety instruction occurs.

title: Ancient China Bingo
rationale: This is a reading and bingo lesson with an incidental prepared tasting, not hands-on cooking. Neither Tasting nor Kitchen & food safety qualifies.

title: Food from the Eastern Hemisphere
rationale: Sesame cauliflower and fried rice entail preparing sauce, cutting and grating vegetables, measuring and mixing from recipes, roasting cauliflower, and stir-frying rice. The rice is pre-cooked, while the closing taste discussion and routine tool use do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Fruit Salad Challenge
rationale: Students cut, portion, and mix fruit, then conduct a blind comparison using tasting vocabulary and a worksheet, which qualifies as structured Tasting. No explicit safety lesson is taught, and students intentionally do not follow a recipe.

title: Go, Grow, Glow
rationale: The burrito bowls entail simmering and sautéing seasoned beans, making tomato-corn salsa, cutting and measuring ingredients, mixing components from recipes, and assembling the bowls. Rice cooking and the chopped onion and garlic are pre-class prep; no structured Tasting or explicit safety instruction occurs.

title: 5th Grade Food Cultures Unit Overview
rationale: Across the six hands-on lessons, vareniki, dumplings, plov, plantain fries, chutney, and bionico entail cutting, measuring, mixing, assembling and wrapping foods, sauce preparation, baking, simmering, sautéing, and steaming from recipes. Advance cooking supplies the first class only; eating afterward is not structured Tasting, and no explicit safety lesson is described.

title: Chickpea Salad
rationale: Students chop vegetables, make a tahini dressing, and mix the chickpea salad. Dressing ingredients are pre-measured, and the preference vote and ordinary tool use do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Pico de Gallo (Knife Skills)
rationale: Students receive dedicated knife-safety instruction, practice specific cuts, and mix chopped produce, lime, and salt into salsa. The final preference vote is not structured Tasting, and the take-home recipe is not the students' cooking guide.

title: African American Foodways: Hoppin' John
rationale: Hoppin' John and cornbread entail dicing and sautéing vegetables, simmering rice and peas, and measuring and mixing cornbread batter from recipes before baking. The peas are par-cooked, but students complete the dishes; eating and general supervision do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Vegetable Ramen
rationale: Students boil noodles, prepare broth, cut and grate toppings, measure ingredients, make furikake seasoning, and assemble ramen bowls using task and recipe cards. The broth alone is pre-made; the meal discussion and general classroom rules do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Healthy Snacks (Energy Bites, Popcorn, Cracker Stack)
rationale: Students follow station directions to measure and roll energy bites, blend seasonings into popcorn, and assemble cracker stacks. Apples are pre-cut, popcorn is pre-popped, and the dough is pre-made; preference voting and routine cautions do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: 3rd Grade Chinese-Style Dumplings
rationale: Students follow task cards to cut, grate, mash, measure, and mix the filling before filling, folding, and sealing dumplings. Steaming is explicitly assigned to the teacher, while the Kitchen Pledge and final eating do not meet the Kitchen & food safety or Tasting thresholds.

title: A Visit to Mashama Bailey's Restaurant
rationale: Students grate garlic, cut collards, measure and stir ingredients from task sheets, and sauté and stew the greens while seasoning prepared grits. Grits and diced onion are pre-class prep; sharing the finished food and supervised stirring do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Bees
rationale: Students make puppets, role-play pollination, and plant seeds; tasting prepared honey does not make this a hands-on cooking lesson. No qualifying Tasting or kitchen-safety skill is practiced.

title: Recycle Gardening
rationale: Students plant seeds in recycled containers rather than prepare food. The optional snack is incidental, with no structured Tasting or kitchen-safety instruction.

title: Seeds
rationale: Students perform a seed dance and save seeds from pods or produce, followed by an incidental tasting. There is no hands-on cooking, structured Tasting, or kitchen-safety lesson.

title: edibleschoolyards_planting
rationale: This lesson consists of plant observation, measurement, and planting, with no food preparation. Tasting and Kitchen & food safety are absent.

title: 2nd Grade Decomposition Experiment Part 1
rationale: Students assemble a soil-decomposition experiment rather than prepare edible food. The closing prepared tasting is incidental, and no qualifying kitchen-safety content is taught.

title: Gardening & The Environment
rationale: Students rotate through gardening jobs and eat a prepared tasting, but perform no hands-on cooking. Garden tool rules do not constitute Kitchen & food safety, and the tasting is unstructured.

title: The Garden in the Spring
rationale: Students search the garden for signs of spring and do not prepare food. Neither Tasting nor Kitchen & food safety is practiced.

title: September Sixth Grade Summer Plant Hunt
rationale: Students map plants, water the garden, and receive a prepared tasting without cooking. The brief sensory prompts do not override the eat-only rule, and no kitchen-safety lesson occurs.

title: Food of Peru
rationale: Arroz chaufa and locro entail cutting and grating ingredients, measuring and stirring from recipes, stir-frying rice components, and simmering the finished stew. Rice, chicha boiling, parboiled potatoes, and roasted squash are pre-class prep; eating together and ordinary tool use do not qualify as Tasting or Kitchen & food safety.

title: Foods From Around the World: China
rationale: Smashed cucumber salad entails smashing and cutting cucumbers, cutting herbs, grating garlic, measuring and mixing dressing, and following the table recipe. The shared tasting is not a structured sensory exercise, and general health reminders do not qualify as Kitchen & food safety.

title: Expert's Guide to Gardening: Compost
rationale: Students observe worms and compost and complete a workbook page rather than cook. The seasonal tasting is incidental, with no structured Tasting or kitchen-safety instruction.
